message:
fix(layout): use INFINITY/NEG_INFINITY for bounding box initialization

Fix 4 instances where bounding box accumulators used f32::MIN/f32::MAX
incorrectly. f32::MIN is the smallest *positive* normal float (~1e-38),
not negative infinity, so empty-element bounding boxes would produce
huge negative dimensions instead of being detected as uninitialized.

This caused:
- Empty diagrams to produce SVG viewBox="0 0 -inf -inf"
- Clusters with no visible members to have corrupt bounding boxes
- Force-directed layout normalization to fail on edge cases

Fixed in compute_bounds(), normalize_positions(), build_cluster_boxes(),
and build_cycle_cluster_results(). All now correctly use f32::INFINITY
for min accumulators and f32::NEG_INFINITY for max accumulators, which
are properly caught by the existing is_finite() guard clauses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
